page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes a dedicated Intel processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance does not include local storage, bundled GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory throughput. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1554.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0, both representing average tier performance. However, multi-core performance is limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1600.79 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2036.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, measuring 15.05 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 9.22 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

The hardware supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for vector and matrix operations, alongside cryptography accelerations like AES and SHA_NI. Despite these advanced instructions, database and web serving benchmarks are low, with Redis SET operations at 2.24 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 3.69 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that require a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances but do not demand high multi-core compute density.

This instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, basic database operations, single-threaded text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and 16 GB of RAM without requiring intensive parallel processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel processor with 1 physical core, 2 threads, and a 260 MB L3 cache, this instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1640.0, but poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 1600.79 and uncached read bandwidth of 15.05 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive, low-compute workloads, making it suitable for lightweight databases, single-threaded text processing, and small-scale memory-bound applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, designed for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine with a high memory-to-core ratio, designed for lightweight memory-intensive applications.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Average single-core performance with limited multi-core scaling
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Suited for lightweight databases and memory-bound single-threaded tasks
